2603.16738.title: MedCL-Bench: Benchmarking stability-efficiency trade-offs and scaling in biomedical continual learning
2603.16738.paper_summary: Medical language models must be updated as evidence and terminology evolve, yet sequential updating can trigger catastrophic forgetting. Although biomedical NLP has many static benchmarks, no unified, task-diverse benchmark exists for evaluating continual learning under standardized protocols, robustness to task order and compute-aware reporting. We introduce MedCL-Bench, which streams ten biomedical NLP datasets spanning five task families and evaluates eleven continual learning strategies across eight task orders, reporting retention, transfer, and GPU-hour cost. Across backbones and task orders, direct sequential fine-tuning on incoming tasks induces catastrophic forgetting, causing update-induced performance regressions on prior tasks. Continual learning methods occupy distinct retention-compute frontiers: parameter-isolation provides the best retention per GPU-hour, replay offers strong protection at higher cost, and regularization yields limited benefit. Forgetting is task-dependent, with multi-label topic classification most vulnerable and constrained-output tasks more robust. MedCL-Bench provides a reproducible framework for auditing model updates before deployment.
2603.16738.paper_summary_zh: 醫學語言模型必須隨著證據和術語的演變而更新，但連續更新可能會引發災難性遺忘。雖然生物醫學自然語言處理有許多靜態基準，但目前尚不存在統一的、任務多樣的基準來評估在標準化協議下的持續學習、對任務順序的穩健性以及計算資源意識的報告。我們介紹了 MedCL-Bench，它串流十個涵蓋五個任務類別的生物醫學 NLP 數據集，並在八個任務順序中評估十一種持續學習策略，報告保留率、轉移率和 GPU 小時成本。在骨幹和任務順序中，對進來的任務進行直接的連續微調會引發災難性遺忘，導致對先前任務的更新引起性能回退。持續學習方法佔據了不同的保留-計算前沿：參數隔離在每個 GPU 小時提供最佳保留，重播在更高成本下提供強有力的保護，而正則化的收益有限。遺忘是任務依賴的，多標籤主題分類最脆弱，而限制輸出任務則更為穩健。MedCL-Bench 提供了一個可重複的框架，用於在部署前審核模型更新。
2603.16738.author: Min Zeng et.al.
2603.16738.authors: Min Zeng, Shuang Zhou, Zaifu Zhan, Rui Zhang
